title: Cross-sell: Customer Hub + K:Social v1
summary: Does contextually surfacing Customer Hub and K:Social cross-sell placements across 9 in-app surfaces drive incremental trial starts without disrupting core workflows?
hypothesis: We believe that adding contextual Customer Hub and K:Social cross-sell placements (header pill popovers + a home banner) for established Klaviyo accounts (28+ days old, both free and paid plans) will drive incremental trial starts on Customer Hub and K:Social because users who have settled into Klaviyo's core flows have an established baseline of value and are in a position to notice and act on adjacent product offerings — and the placements are surfaced on pages where the cross-sell value prop is contextually relevant (e.g., Subscriber Growth for K:Social, Profiles/Forms for CH retention). We will measure success using trial-start rate per variant (CH trial starts for customer_hub arm, K:Social trial starts for ksocial arm — primary metric per arm), and monitor placement CTA click-through rate, dismissal rate, downstream onboarding completion of trial accounts, and engagement on the host page.
notes: Two separate Statsig experiments run in parallel — prx-ksocial-cross-sell-v1-relaunch and prx-ch-cross-sell-v1-relaunch. Design screenshots attached in the Slack thread reply (3 images). Targeting 20% of eligible accounts ramping up, on Stripe billing. Linear: https://linear.app/klaviyo/project/cross-sell-customer-hub-ksocial-v1-5a0439b9273b
control_description: Standard Klaviyo app — no cross-sell placements
treatment_description: Dismissible promo for Customer Hub or K:Social across 9 in-app placements (Profiles, Lists & Segments header + bottom blur row, Flow create, Sign-up Forms, Integrations header + Discover apps grid, Home banner, Subscriber Growth); dual-eligible users split evenly between arms
